body_txt: Kings, queens, knights, serfs, fiefdoms... and rocket ships. This trope is Feudalism IN SPACE!!! While these Science Fiction stories are set in distant planets in a world where space travel and futuristic technology is available, the people are still ruled under a medieval-style feudal system. Kings and queens, princes and princesses , nobles , courts , Knights (in Powered Armor or Humongous Mecha ).... A form of Days of Future Past which can incorporate elements from the The High Middle Ages right up to the Victorian Age . The chief characteristic will be that noble social status is legally enforced and hereditary . Occasionally we will be told that the king/emperor is elected , but it makes no difference as to their authority, and issues regarding who the electors are or who is eligible to run never seem to come up. This is among the commonest societies in Space Opera , Planetary Romance , and other forms of Science Fiction. It is also common in post-apocalyptic stories , where the loss of modern technology and infrastructure is mirrored by a return to historic forms of governance. In certain cases, future society may regress past even a feudal or imperial historic state and return to living in conditions reminescent of the hunter-gatherers and early agriculturalists of prehistoric times, or even return to a pre-sapient state altogether and live as apes in the wilderness; for cases of these situations, see Future Primitive and Formerly Sapient Species . Falls into two categories: Prone to Medieval Stasis , even though technology is far above medieval level. May also involve anachronistic items from real medieval Technology Levels . Evil nobles may restrict commoners' use of high technology; medical technology is particularly common, but commoners often live lives of drudgery and toil . The extent of which any of it can be considered "feudal" is up for grabs. Often an excuse to use Medieval European Fantasy tropes or tropes from other Common Historical Settings in SF. In terms of morality, futuristic feudal governments may range all the way from rather benevolent to Aristocrats Are Evil and Decadent Court . However, even if clearly dystopian , the authorities following this trope are seldom kept in place by naked force, 1984 -style. Social status is legally inherited, and the royals and nobles draw their authority from the law, even if said law is very blatant about depriving everyone who isn't born into a noble family of all political power. This often leans towards the Romantic end of Romanticism Versus Enlightenment . In some works, heroes have great ease in converting them to democracies. Partly because writers seem to be unaware of any arguments against democracy, and of the complexity of developing a stable democracy. Discussions of in-universe reasons for feudalism should go on the analysis page .
